name: Egg Pancake Rolls with Pork Filling
recipeIngredient: 1 cup (about ½ lb) ground pork, uncooked
2 Scallions, chopped fine (white parts separate from the green)
2 teaspoons Cornstarch, dissolved in 2 tablespoons Water, cold
Dash hot chili oil or Tabasco
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, stir the eggs with the dry sherry.
2. In a mixing bowl, combine all the filling ingredients, including only the white parts of the chopped scallions (save the green parts for garnish) and stir to blend well.
3. In an 8-inch skillet, heat the oil lightly. Pour about a quarter of the egg mixture to cover the bottom of the skillet. Heat until the egg pancake has barely set, then transfer to a plate.
4. Make 3 more pancakes using the remaining egg mixture.
5. Divide the filling into 4 portions and place one portion on each pancake.
6. Moisten your fingers with cold water and spread the filling mixture evenly over the surface of the pancake.
7. Roll up the pancakes tightly, as you would a jelly roll. Seal with a little leftover egg from the egg-beating bowl.
8. Place the rolls on a flat plate that will fit in your steamer, then steam them over an inch of simmering water for 15 minutes.
9. In a bowl, mix together the sauce ingredients.
10. To serve, cut the rolls into slices, cutting at an angle. Drizzle the sauce over them and top with the scallion greens and carrots. Makes 6 to 8 appetizer servings.
